text: The laparoscopic view shows the surgeon performing total mesorectal excision (TME) to mobilize the rectum down to the pelvic floor, with precise dissection of surrounding tissues.
The surgeon divides the peritoneum bilaterally, demonstrating the mobility of the rectum on the right side and completing the division on the patient's left side.
The surgeon releases the peritoneal attachments of the sigmoid colon, straightening it out. The rectum is visibly mobile within the surgical field.
The surgeon uses protacks to secure the peritoneum to the sacral promontory bilaterally, three times. The rectum is visibly hitched onto the sacral promontory, resolving the prolapse.
